data.body:
[G3 child-coverage — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S6 1ba5: Docs — private-repo credentials, descriptive errors, ref/source semantics, HMAC trust model); audited against the WHOLE EIGHT-child roster]

VERDICT: EMPTY G3 findings list. 4-bucket audit of this Docs child's contribution to the parent's 9 acceptance criteria + the success-criteria narrative; every absence cross-checked against the WHOLE roster (S1 6d56 materialize core, S2 70c7 cache, S2b b73c janitor, S3 fa49 gate threading, S4 30b3 attested signing, S4b e308 claim-gate ref-coherence/ADR 0002, S5 b7fa CLI/MCP surfacing). The union of children covers every parent criterion; no uncovered parent criterion is attributable to this pairing.

4-bucket coverage map (parent AC -> covering child/children; THIS docs child's role + bucket):
- AC1 (no attested gate reads the working tree; all read a faithful snapshot at the pinned SHA) — FULLY by S1 (faithful read-tree+checkout-index) + S3 (gate re-rooting). This child DOCUMENTS the attested/ref semantics (its AC3). Bucket STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this docs child (Scope: "Out: code (S1-S5)"). Not an absence.
- AC2 (signed verdict binds verified_at_sha via the signed MANIFEST CHANNEL — no payload-schema/version change, prior signatures verify — reproducible against that SHA) — FULLY by S4 (30b3 AC1/AC2) atop S1's immutable-SHA basis. This child is the DOCS owner of the trust surface: AC3 (signs-vs-never-signs + verified_at_sha recording for CLI+MCP) + AC4 (HMAC trust model + limits + in-toto Statement shape), explicitly delegated by S4 ("limits documented in S6"). Bucket PARTIALLY (docs half; binding behavior S4). Not an absence.
- AC3 (local mode reads in-place checkout AND cannot produce a signed verdict; source=local is the documented back-out) — by S3 (in-place read + advisory AC "source=local documented as the rollback to in-place reads") + S4 (signing bar). This child DOCUMENTS attested-signs/local-never-signs + fail-closed-attested/local-still-runs (its AC2/AC3). Bucket PARTIALLY. The back-out clause has a named home in S3's advisory AC, so not an absence.
- AC4 (concurrent gates without git-lock contention/cross-op corruption; cache + janitor reclaim under a defined free-space watermark without disrupting in-flight reads) — FULLY by S1 (atomic population) + S2 (single-flight, delete-on-last-close) + S2b (janitor watermark reclamation). This child DOCUMENTS the disk-cap behavior (its AC5). Bucket STRUCTURAL w.r.t. mechanism. Not an absence.
- AC5 (private-repo fetch failure surfaces a descriptive, actionable credential error) — SPLIT, with THIS child as DOCS PRIMARY OWNER: S1 (6d56 AC5) RAISES the actionable error; this child's AC1 (README MCP-setup + docs/jira-sync-setup.md state the fetch-credential requirement + how to supply it, grep-able) + AC2 (message + remedy + fail-closed-attested/local-still-runs in a named file) own the documentation; the "actionable"/how-to-supply facet is precisely this child's contribution. Bucket FULLY (raise S1 + docs here). Clean split.
- AC6 (portable macOS+Linux: no external tar; no hardcoded /tmp; flock+mkdir fallback) — implementation FULLY by S1; this child DOCUMENTS REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR + the EFS/NFS flock caveat (its AC5, xref alto-fruit-punch). Bucket FULLY (impl S1 + docs here). Clean split.
- AC7 (operational tunables — free-space watermark, default ref, grace/age windows — configurable with documented defaults, S2/S3) — implementation by S2b (b73c AC9 watermark/grace/max-age/reverify) + S3 (fa49 ref/source defaults via standard rebar config + REBAR_* precedence). This child DOCUMENTS the disk-cap/knobs (its AC5) + origin/main default + ref/source semantics (its AC3). Bucket PARTIALLY (documented-defaults prose facet co-owned; each tunable child carries its own documented-defaults AC). Not an absence.
- AC8 (an ADR captures the architecture + rejected alternatives — PID-lease, payload-schema SHA binding) — authored by S2/S2b (b73c AC10 rejected PID+heartbeat lease) + S4 (30b3 AC1 manifest-vs-payload SHA-binding decision) + S4b (e308 ADR 0002). Bucket STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this PROSE-docs child (the ADR is "authored in S2/S4" per the parent note; Scope "Out: code (S1-S5)"). Both named rejected alternatives have an authoring home. Not an absence.
- AC9 (all EIGHT enumerated child stories closed; make lint + full test suite green) — STRUCTURAL/meta epic-completion criterion; covered by the existence of all eight children (this child is the docs story, 1 of 8) + each child's own green-bar obligation. Not an absence at this child.

Success-criteria narrative ("Every code-reading gate verifies a client-specified, immutable, reproducible snapshot at a pinned SHA — never the server's mutable checkout — safely under concurrent distributed use; signed verdicts bind verified_at_sha via the manifest channel (reproducible, no prior-signature breakage); local mode preserves the single-developer verify-before-push workflow") decomposes onto AC1-AC6, all behaviorally owned by S1-S5/S2b. This child carries the operator/agent-facing DOCUMENTATION surface the snapshot model newly requires — the fetch-credential prerequisite, the descriptive failure behavior, the attested-signs/local-never-signs + origin/main + verified_at_sha semantics, the HMAC trust model + limits + in-toto Statement shape, the REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR/disk-cap/EFS-NFS-flock knobs. It introduces NO behavioral obligation of its own and leaves no clause uncovered.

Named-consumer / delegation cross-checks (covered-by-named-consumer, not absences):
- S4 (30b3) "What"/AC explicitly delegates the HMAC-trust-model + in-toto Statement-shape docs to S6 ("limits documented in S6") — claimed here by this child's AC4.
- S1 (6d56 AC5) defers the descriptive credential MESSAGE detail to S6 ("see S6") — claimed here by this child's AC1/AC2.
- S5 (b7fa) scopes prose docs out ("Out: ... prose docs (S6)"); the ref/source PROSE semantics are claimed here by AC3 — a clean schema-text-vs-prose split (S5 owns inline outputSchema/text + documented defaults; this child owns the human prose in named, grep-able files). No double-ownership, no union-coverage gap.
Each cross-ref resolves to a concrete AC on this child; no docs obligation named by a sibling is left unclaimed.

Doc-surface reality check (so each "is documented (named file)" AC is a deterministic grep-able check, not phantom prose): docs/jira-sync-setup.md exists; README.md exists (repo root); docs/adr/ exists (consistent with the ADR authored by S2/S4/S4b). The named files this child pins its ACs to are real.

Note (scope, NOT a G3 finding): the standing S3<->S4/S4b dependency-gap on routing the pinned-SHA snapshot handle into plan_review/attest.py's dependency_hashes()/_rehash()/freshness re-check is a CONSISTENCY (G4) code seam between S3/S4/S4b (and a G6 mechanism note at the parent); it does not touch this docs child and leaves no parent criterion unclaimed (AC1/AC2/ref-coherence remain claimed by children).

Conclusion: this child cleanly covers the documentation slice of the parent criteria — it is the docs PRIMARY OWNER of parent AC5 (credential requirement + actionable remedy, grep-able) and the docs half of AC2/AC3/AC4/AC6/AC7. No parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered by the roster. No G3 finding for this pairing.
